title: The Daughter Also Rises

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Opening of episode with Homer's signature exclamation
jokes.punchline: The iconic D'oh! as episode opener
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase - perfect character integration and economy, but low originality since it's overused by this point

jokes.text: Happy 500th episode! / This is episode 499. / Aw, geez. Well, I got news for ya, Fox ain't doin' this again.
jokes.punchline: Realizes it's only 499 and Fox won't do this celebration again
jokes.explanation: Smart meta joke about the show's own production, with good structure and topical relevance

jokes.text: How'd you get so many more Valentines? / I got one from Lisa and one from my optometrist. / That's a bill. / No, it's got a love stamp!
jokes.punchline: Homer mistakes a bill for a valentine due to the stamp
jokes.explanation: Well-structured joke about Homer's naivety, solid character work

jokes.text: Last night you had the night terrors. / Yeah, I dreamt I got fired from that job I dreamt I got the night before.
jokes.punchline: Homer's nested dream about getting fired from a dream job
jokes.explanation: Brilliant nested dream concept with excellent absurdist structure - this is genuinely clever writing

jokes.text: Welcome to MythCrackers, where we debunk rumors, legends, and all religions except the Jesus-based ones.
jokes.punchline: Exception for Christian religions in their debunking
jokes.explanation: Sharp satirical take on TV science shows and religious bias in media

jokes.text: Luckily, she doesn't know that our viewing platforms are... multi. / No TV on the computer! / No TV on a smart phone!
jokes.setup: Homer thinks he's clever about multiple devices
jokes.punchline: Marge immediately catches and bans each platform
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure showing Marge staying ahead of Homer's schemes

jokes.text: Now, we didn't want to hurt a real cat, so we took this Build-A-Bear carcass... Stuffed it with ballistic gel, shot it with a 20 foot barrel steam cannon... And made a scatter plot of the remains!
jokes.setup: Ethical concern about not hurting real animals
jokes.punchline: Extremely violent scientific method on stuffed animal
jokes.explanation: Good parody of MythBusters' over-the-top methodology with dark twist

jokes.text: What was it we trying to prove again? / Don't know, don't care!
jokes.punchline: Complete disregard for scientific method
jokes.explanation: Sharp critique of spectacle over substance in science TV shows

jokes.text: That might be a good idea, but then again, you thought it up.
jokes.punchline: Bart's backhanded dismissal of Milhouse's intelligence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart-Milhouse dynamic with efficient put-down

jokes.text: We show girls love on Valentine's Day, and they let us blow stuff up on the Fourth of July. I just pray they never fall on the same day.
jokes.setup: Homer explains the Valentine's Day dynamic
jokes.punchline: Fear of holidays coinciding
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about gender dynamics and Homer's simple worldview

jokes.text: You have the most wonderful mom in the world, which is why I have no idea where she is or what she's doing.
jokes.punchline: Admits complete ignorance about her activities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - praising while revealing complete obliviousness

jokes.text: I mean, my darling, I was looking at myself in the side view mirror.
jokes.punchline: Quickly covers by claiming he was talking to himself
jokes.explanation: Classic Moe awkwardness and poor cover-up skills

jokes.text: I hope you hit one off the handle and your hands sting! / Ow!
jokes.setup: Marge's sarcastic curse on Homer's batting
jokes.punchline: Immediate fulfillment with Homer's pain
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing with instant karma structure

jokes.text: Someone's about to put the Thousand Island ladle into the vinaigrette!
jokes.setup: Lisa and Marge at salad bar looking for commonalities
jokes.punchline: Lisa's food-mixing emergency
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - intellectual precision about trivial matters

jokes.text: It was dark, then a light came through the crack. The light was you.
jokes.setup: Nick explaining how he noticed Lisa
jokes.punchline: Overly poetic description of seeing through restaurant booth crack
jokes.explanation: Good setup for pretentious teen character with overly flowery language

jokes.text: Words are just nails we use to build ideas.
jokes.setup: Lisa complimenting Nick's way with words
jokes.punchline: Pretentious metaphor about words and ideas
jokes.explanation: Perfect pseudo-intellectual teenage pretension - quotable in its ridiculousness

jokes.text: Don't panic. This can be separated out. Quick, does anyone have a centrifuge?!
jokes.punchline: Lisa's over-the-top scientific solution
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of Lisa's scientific mindset applied to trivial problem

jokes.text: Yep, you're the crack head.
jokes.setup: Homer saying he wants a romantic night
jokes.punchline: Marge's double-entendre about the bathroom crack
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay with drug reference, economical delivery

jokes.text: I'll toss this fork into the air and wherever it lands, that's what I'll eat. Hiya!
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous method of food selection with ninja sound effect
jokes.explanation: Decent character beat showing Nick's quirky pretensions

jokes.text: When I first hear your name, I want to be somewhere important, the peak of Kilimanjaro... the back of a vaporetto in Venice... Lisa! Now this is the most important place in the world.
jokes.setup: Nick wants to hear Lisa's name somewhere special
jokes.punchline: Reframes ordinary restaurant as most important place
jokes.explanation: Well-structured romantic gesture that's both sweet and appropriately over-the-top for teen character

jokes.text: Now that's a spicy meet-cute! Mwah!
jokes.setup: Witnessing Lisa and Nick's first meeting
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'spicy' meatball and romantic meet-cute
jokes.explanation: Serviceable pun but relies on obvious wordplay without much craft

jokes.text: That cost a nickel, but, uh, we'll settle up later.
jokes.punchline: Treating tiny amount as debt to settle later
jokes.explanation: Good Fat Tony character moment treating trivial amounts with mob boss seriousness

jokes.text: Did you invent a robot hand to touch the buttons? / Something like that.
jokes.punchline: Bart uses Milhouse instead of robot hand
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart-Milhouse dynamic with good visual setup and efficient execution

jokes.text: They were discontinued because they were thought to encourage impressionable children to smoke. / How dumb do they think we are?
jokes.punchline: Kids immediately start 'smoking' them while asking how dumb they think kids are
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic structure proving the point while denying it - solid social commentary

jokes.text: I can quit any time I want!
jokes.punchline: Classic addiction denial phrase about candy
jokes.explanation: Good callback that escalates the addiction parallel

jokes.text: If I cover my peripheral vision, I feel like I'm in France. / How 'bout I just look at you.
jokes.setup: Nick's romantic technique about feeling like in France
jokes.punchline: Lisa's sweet but pointed redirect
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Lisa's practicality vs Nick's affectation

jokes.text: El Puerco, I must break up with you. Oh, but the hand-holding is just too good.
jokes.setup: Nick dramatically breaking up with his meal
jokes.punchline: Unable to resist the tactile experience
jokes.explanation: Funny absurdist moment showing Nick's dramatic personality applied to food

jokes.text: Perhaps we can try a little harder, for the lady. / Such is the life of the juice boxiér.
jokes.punchline: Nick creates pretentious job title for juice box server
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that perfectly captures Nick's pretentious character while being genuinely funny

jokes.text: Would you like to borrow one of my twins? But remember, the one you pick may not be the one you get.
jokes.setup: Offering to lend Marge a twin
jokes.punchline: Mysterious warning about twin selection
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd premise that leaves audience wondering what this could possibly mean

jokes.text: No excuse, I just forgot all about you.
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest admission instead of excuse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment - choosing uncomfortable honesty over social niceties

jokes.text: Well, if you'd been here for the design phase, you could've registered that objection. Now start quilting!
jokes.setup: Lisa objects to her patch design
jokes.punchline: Marge's corporate-speak response about quilting
jokes.explanation: Funny juxtaposition of business jargon applied to domestic crafts

jokes.text: Cool! Now my beauty will be on the outside!
jokes.setup: Testing myth about swings turning body inside-out
jokes.punchline: Milhouse excited about beauty being externalized
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse self-deprecation - acknowledges his looks while maintaining optimism

jokes.text: Is it on your father? Little girls always get crushes on their fathers. / Somehow I missed that phase.
jokes.punchline: Lisa's dry acknowledgment of skipping normal development
jokes.explanation: Great character beat showing Lisa's awareness of her unusual psychology

jokes.text: Okay. Let's 'esque' him to dinner.
jokes.punchline: Marge turns suffix into verb
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay showing Marge trying to connect with Lisa's vocabulary

jokes.text: This house is not an embarrassment! / Hm, usually when I say something like that, something extremely embarrassing happens. I'm gonna say good night before it does.
jokes.punchline: Immediate recognition of sitcom pattern and attempt to avoid it
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor where Lisa demonstrates awareness of comedy conventions and tries to subvert them

jokes.text: Well, Lisa didn't tell me that she had a sister. / Or a daughter.
jokes.punchline: Confused about whether Marge is Lisa's sister or has a daughter
jokes.explanation: Standard confusion joke about family relationships

jokes.text: Well, your arms are lanky. It's kind of a long trip down there.
jokes.punchline: Marge's practical observation about arm length
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge response - deflating romantic gesture with practical observation

jokes.text: Hmm, the French have gotten into the wine game? Ha, good luck catchin' up with the big boys.
jokes.punchline: Homer's ignorant dismissal of France's wine heritage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer ignorance - dismissing France as wine newcomer shows his complete lack of knowledge

jokes.text: If you do, it'll mean you're a separate person from me. / That'll stop her from seeing him.
jokes.setup: Marge telling Lisa not to spend time with Nick
jokes.punchline: Marge's self-aware recognition of reverse psychology
jokes.explanation: Smart parenting humor showing Marge's understanding of teenage psychology

jokes.text: Yeah, there's a lot of fish in the sea, but she picked out a drunken walrus.
jokes.punchline: Grampa's harsh but accurate description of Homer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of 'fish in the sea' cliché with vivid, accurate Homer description

jokes.text: I don't like to babble on, but I sure do like Babylon.
jokes.setup: Grampa telling story of Pyramus and Thisbe
jokes.punchline: Pun on Babylon/babble while telling ancient story
jokes.explanation: Decent pun that fits Grampa's rambling storytelling character

jokes.text: Talk to the wing, Thisbe!
jokes.punchline: Disney parrot making 'talk to the hand' pun with wing
jokes.explanation: Formulaic Disney parody with predictable pun - functional but uninspired

jokes.text: Let me check my schedule. This might work. I think I'm free. Tuesday's open. You got it, kiddo!
jokes.setup: Lisa asking for help with romantic mission
jokes.punchline: Grampa pretending to have busy schedule
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Grampa's dignity despite obvious availability

jokes.text: The one day the lunch lady decides to wash her hands.
jokes.punchline: Blaming failure on lunch lady's rare hygiene
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about school food service hygiene standards

jokes.text: It's a Twix. / I can't be the guy who killed everybody's fun. That's Skinner's job.
jokes.punchline: Bart's existential crisis about being fun-killer like Skinner
jokes.explanation: Good character development showing Bart's self-awareness about his role

jokes.text: American Girl doll day?! / You might be surprised at the number of boys who have American Girl dolls. It's not weird 'cause they're historic figures.
jokes.setup: Milhouse excited about American Girl dolls
jokes.punchline: Defensive justification about historical significance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse moment - enthusiasm plus defensive rationalization

jokes.text: I'm not from Edinburgh! I'm also not from Glasgow! I'm from Kirkwall in Orkney! Me father was an Uppie, and me mother was a Doonie. It tore the family apart!
jokes.setup: Willie correcting myths about his origin
jokes.punchline: Obscure Scottish rivalry causing family drama
jokes.explanation: Excellent Willie moment with authentic Scottish references and escalating family tragedy

jokes.text: Hola! Yo soy Maury Povich. Uno de estos siete hombres es el verdadero padre de este 'crack baby.'
jokes.punchline: Spanish Maury Povich show about paternity
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist moment playing on language barriers and daytime TV

jokes.text: Chief, we heard gunfire! / Relax! It's just my back! And my hip. Spinal column!
jokes.punchline: Chief Wiggum's body parts cracking loudly
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy escalation with Wiggum's aging body

jokes.text: Yeah, I don't normally approve of out-breeding, but you two seem nice.
jokes.setup: Cletus giving Lisa and Nick a ride
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to inbreeding preferences
jokes.explanation: Perfect Cletus character moment with casual dark humor

jokes.text: Did you say 'ultimate Frisbee'?!
jokes.punchline: Homer's enthusiastic mishearing as 'ultimate Frisbee'
jokes.explanation: Good Homer mishearing joke showing his interests vs classical literature

jokes.text: Women and shoes, am I right?
jokes.setup: Women struggling with footwear on boat
jokes.punchline: Homer's generic male observation
jokes.explanation: Generic stereotype humor that relies on predictable gender observation

jokes.text: Women and shoes, am I right?
jokes.setup: Marge struggling with boat in heels
jokes.punchline: Homer's generic gender stereotype comment
jokes.explanation: Weak generic stereotype humor, lacks originality but fits character

jokes.text: I sure was-- I'm allergic to chocolate!
jokes.punchline: Reveals cowardice about chocolate allergy
jokes.explanation: Decent character deflation showing Nick's pretensions vs reality

jokes.text: Lisa, it's Hadley Richardson, Hemingway's first wife. Trust me, you're making a big mistake.
jokes.punchline: Literary ghost warning about writer boyfriends
jokes.explanation: Clever literary reference with good setup for warnings about writers

jokes.text: He used to pee in the fireplace!
jokes.punchline: Fish's gross personal detail about Hemingway
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out detail that deflates romantic writer mythology

jokes.text: Lisa, I'm sorry that God gave me this gift of lying to girls, for a little while.
jokes.punchline: Pretentious excuse about divine gift of deception
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nick character moment - turning dishonesty into spiritual calling

jokes.text: Ooh, you know, I've heard if a fat guy stops moving, he floats.
jokes.punchline: Marge's scientific observation about Homer's buoyancy
jokes.explanation: Good Marge deadpan delivery with practical observation about Homer's physique

tmdb_overview: Marge and Lisa's plans for a mother-daughter Valentine's Day take a turn when Lisa falls head over heels for an intellectual romantic; Bart and Milhouse investigate Springfield Elementary's legends.
